author: Peter Hecke
message:
Phase 3 Batch 2: Complete Activities and Relationships categories - 21 samples migrated

Migrated Entity Operations samples (Activities + Relationships) from legacy .NET Framework 4.7.2 to modern .NET 6+ architecture.

## Activities Category (16 samples)
- BookAppointment: Book appointments using BookRequest
- BulkEmail: Send bulk emails and monitor async operations
- ConvertFaxToTask: Convert fax activities to tasks
- ConvertToRecurring: Convert appointments to recurring series
- CreateEmailUsingTemplate: Create emails from templates
- CRUDEmailAttachments: CRUD operations on email attachments
- CRUDRecurringAppointment: CRUD operations on recurring appointments
- CustomActivity: Create and manage custom activity entities
- EmailTemplate: Work with email templates
- EndRecurringAppointment: End recurring appointment series
- PromoteEmail: Promote emails to create related records
- RecurringAppointment: Create recurring appointment series
- RetrieveEmailAttach: Retrieve email attachments
- SendEmail: Send email messages
- SendEmailUsingTemp: Send emails using templates
- ValidateAppointment: Validate appointment scheduling

Note: CollaborateWithActivityFeeds not migrated (deprecated Activity Feeds feature)

## Relationships Category (5 samples)
- AssociateRecords: Associate/disassociate records using relationships
- ConnectionEarlyBound: Create connections between records
- ConnectionRole: Create and manage connection roles
- ReciprocalConnection: Create reciprocal connections
- UpdateConnectionRole: Update connection role definitions

## Key Changes Applied
- Replaced CrmServiceClient with ServiceClient
- Updated to Microsoft.PowerPlatform.Dataverse.Client package
- Converted all early-bound entities to late-bound Entity syntax
- Implemented Setup/Run/Cleanup pattern with entityStore tracking
- Updated namespace to PowerPlatform.Dataverse.CodeSamples
- Uses appsettings.json for configuration
- All samples build successfully with 0 errors

Total: 21 samples migrated successfully

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
